pricing.source: OpenAI API pricing, standard short-context rates, 2026-06-23

hardChecks.label: Citation source ids
hardChecks.expectation: Every cited source id exists in the source packet

hardChecks.label: Cost metadata
hardChecks.expectation: estimatedUsd is present when pricing is configured

hardChecks.label: Token metadata
hardChecks.expectation: Input, cached-input, and output token counts are coherent

hardChecks.label: Provider and model metadata
hardChecks.expectation: Provider, model, and prompt version are present

hardChecks.label: Judge input boundary
hardChecks.expectation: Judge prompt omits eval-only object keys and holdout tuning fields

hardChecks.label: Judge claim coverage
hardChecks.expectation: LLM judge returns exactly one judgment for every briefing claim

claimJudgments.claimText: Candidate prompt increases median token usage by ~11% but improves latency by ~6% due to fewer manual reruns (not shorter generations).
claimJudgments.explanation: S1 explicitly states median token usage increased by 11% and latency improved by 6%, attributing the latency improvement to fewer repeated/manual reruns rather than shorter generations.

claimJudgments.claimText: Finance will accept up to a 1.15x cost ratio for demo-quality runs if extra tokens are justified by reduced review time; above that requires smaller batches, stricter retry caps, or more aggressive trace summarization.
claimJudgments.explanation: S2 sets a 1.15x cost ceiling conditioned on explaining review-time reduction benefits; it also specifies that above-threshold runs need smaller batches, stricter retry caps, or more aggressive trace summarization.

claimJudgments.claimText: Reviewers find long traces useful only when summarized next to the failing claim; raw provider payloads slow review because evidence is hard to locate.
claimJudgments.explanation: S3 says long traces help only when summarized next to the failed claim and that raw payload dumps slow review because reviewers must search for relevant evidence.

claimJudgments.claimText: Live demo felt responsive when a single briefing completes under 10 seconds and progress shows the current case; longer full runs are acceptable if progress/artifact paths update continuously.
claimJudgments.explanation: S4 specifies the under-10-second single-briefing target and that longer full runs were tolerated when UI continuously showed current case/progress and artifact path updates.

claimJudgments.claimText: Draft policy: allow one automatic retry for transient provider errors; require a manifest entry when retries change cost/latency; avoid silent repeated generations.
claimJudgments.explanation: S5 proposes a small retry cap with one automatic retry for transient provider errors, requires a manifest entry when retries change cost/latency, and rejects silent repeated generations.

missingImportantEvidence: S6 trace summary mock preference for dashboard summaries + keeping detailed traces on disk (recommendation uses this idea but does not cite S6)
recommendationJudgment.explanation: The recommendation to keep claim-level trace summaries in the UI, store full raw traces on disk, and cap retries to one transient-error retry with manifest logging aligns with S3/S5 and the cost/latency/demo constraints in S1/S2/S4. However, the specific UI content list (selected source ids, missing evidence, retry count, output path) and the 'keep detailed traces on disk while dashboard stays readable' concept are directly supported by S6, which is not cited in the generated briefing’s claims/recommendation section.

citationSupport.note: S1 appears in fully supported claim-level judgments.
S2 appears in fully supported claim-level judgments.
S3 appears in fully supported claim-level judgments.
S4 appears in fully supported claim-level judgments.
S5 appears in fully supported claim-level judgments.

notes: Hybrid evaluator output: deterministic hard checks plus structured LLM judge evidence. Manual spot checks are still required before claiming product improvement.
